$comment: Phase 6.4 session 76 BACKLOG 1.36 closure: KAT golden vectors for audit-log-export cross-language byte-equivalence. SOURCE OF TRUTH is packages/evidence/tests/audit-log-export.test.ts:564-650 (KAT-1, KAT-1b, KAT-2, KAT-3, KAT-4, KAT-5). This file pins KAT-2 (content_hash), KAT-3 (event_hash), and KAT-5 (dual_subtree_daily_root) for Go-side consumer verification. KAT-1/1b skipped (Go side has no event_id derivation primitive — that's a writer-side concern). KAT-4 skipped at first cut (subtree_root requires Go-side buildAuditLogSubtree primitive which doesn't exist; Go verifier validates pre-built proofs at Check 9). If any value below drifts from the TS golden vector, the cross-language contract has BROKEN and the cause must be investigated — NOT just snapshot-updated.

kats_skipped.KAT-1: deriveAuditLogEventId(org, seq=0, ts) — UUID v5 derivation is a WRITER-SIDE primitive; verifier consumes event_id as opaque UUID and validates UUID-shape via the regex check (no recomputation). A third-party implementer writing a verifier would skip KAT-1 identically.
kats_skipped.KAT-1b: deriveAuditLogEventId(org, seq=42, ts) — see KAT-1 rationale.
kats_skipped.KAT-4: buildAuditLogSubtree(events, …) — subtree-root construction is a WRITER-SIDE primitive; the verifier validates a pre-built subtree via Merkle proof walks (Check 9 sections 4-7) and dual-subtree composition (KAT-5 below). Coverage transitively via conformance-test fixtures (Go verifier reads a TS-generated bundle and Check 9 passes). Future BACKLOG: Go-side buildAuditLogSubtree primitive + KAT-4 consumer test for defense-in-depth on dual-subtree.

KAT-2.description: computeAuditLogContentHashGo({actor, event_type, subject}) matches TS computeAuditLogContentHash

KAT-3.description: computeEventHashGo(prev=genesis, content_hash=KAT-2, seq=0, ts) matches TS computeAuditLogEventHash
